name: Human
lore: Humans dominate the galaxy's power structures — the Empire, the Senate before it, the corporate boardrooms, and the criminal syndicates. In the Western Reaches, humans are colonists, refugees, opportunists, and exiles. They carry no biological advantages into the frontier, but they carry something more dangerous: the assumption that they belong everywhere.

arenaShift.name: The Baseline
arenaShift.desc: All Arenas at D6. No biological peaks or penalties.

favoredDiscipline.desc: Choose any one discipline as your favored discipline (exploding die + Edge reroll).

biologicalTruth.name: Galactic Ubiquity
biologicalTruth.desc: Humans make up the vast majority of the Empire, the corporate sector, and the underworld. In Imperial-controlled settings, you can blend into crowds, acquire standard-issue gear, and move through human-centric organizations without drawing the prejudice, suspicion, or xenophobia that alien species face. (GM-gated: the social lubricant works where the Empire's Humanocentrism holds sway.)

speciesTrait.name: Adaptable
speciesTrait.desc: You gain one free Discipline advance at character creation.

loreAnchors: Beneficiary of Imperial Humanocentrism — Even poor humans face less systemic harassment than aliens.
Galactic Ubiquity — Can blend into Imperial outposts, corporate mining camps, or refugee caravans without drawing innate suspicion.
Adaptable — Begins with a free advance, reflecting the sheer variety of human experience and ambition.
_aiMeta.directives: If the character is anti-Empire, emphasize that they walked away from the privilege of Imperial High Culture. If they are underworld, emphasize how easily they slip past Imperial customs. The free advance should reflect their unique life path.

name: Cathar
lore: The Cathar are a species of feline bipedal humanoids from a wild world of savannas, rough uplands, and vast city-trees carved with the histories of their heroes. Millennia ago, the Mandalorians nearly wiped them out — over ninety percent of the species slaughtered in a single campaign. But the Cathar endured, rebuilt, and by the time of the Galactic Civil War, their world has fully recovered. That genocide is not forgotten; it lives in their clan songs, their carvings, and their refusal to ever be prey again. Cathar are proud, passionate, and moral — they mate for life, fight with everything they have, and hold grudges that outlast starships. In the Western Reaches, a Cathar is the crew member who will not abandon you, will not back down, and will leave claw marks on anything that threatens the people they've claimed as theirs.

arenaShift.name: Feral Tenacity
arenaShift.desc: Grit starts at D8. Wits drops to D4. Fierce, passionate, and nearly impossible to put down — but instinct-driven, not analytical.

favoredDiscipline.desc: Choose one favored discipline (exploding die + Edge reroll).

biologicalTruth.name: Sensitive Hearing
biologicalTruth.desc: Your feline ears are exceptionally acute — sharper than Twi'lek or Human hearing. You can detect whispered conversations at distance, hear footsteps through walls, and pick up on subtle changes in tone that betray a lie or a threat. In quiet environments, you are almost impossible to sneak up on.

speciesTrait.name: Natural Weapons
speciesTrait.desc: You possess retractable razor claws — a natural weapon that cannot be taken from you. You are never considered unarmed, even when stripped of all equipment. Unlike standard unarmed strikes, your claws deal lethal slashing damage rather than stun damage. Your claws can also be used to climb with uncanny speed and confidence.

loreAnchors: Natural Weapons — Retractable claws deal lethal damage; never unarmed, even when stripped of equipment.
Sensitive Hearing — Feline ears detect whispers at distance, footsteps through walls; nearly impossible to sneak up on.
Passionate Loyalty — Cathar mate for life; their bonds to crew, clan, and cause are absolute. They do not abandon their own.
_aiMeta.directives: The character's backstory should reflect the tension between their passionate nature and whatever discipline they've adopted. Cathar Jedi struggle against instinct; Cathar soldiers channel it. The Mandalorian genocide should be a cultural memory — not necessarily a personal trauma at this point in history, but a reason why Cathar never underestimate a threat and never forgive a betrayal. Their claws should be described as part of everyday life — grooming them, sheathing them in polite company, extending them when the situation demands it.

name: Duros
tagline: Born to navigate. Built for the void.
lore: The Duros were among the first species to chart hyperspace routes and build interstellar civilizations. Their culture is one of exploration, wanderlust, and an almost spiritual connection to the stars. In the poorly-mapped Western Reaches — where uncharted hyperspace lanes border the Unknown Regions — a Duros navigator is worth their weight in coaxium.

arenaShift.name: The Baseline
arenaShift.desc: All Arenas at D6. No biological peaks or penalties.

favoredDiscipline.desc: Choose one favored discipline (exploding die + Edge reroll).

biologicalTruth.name: Void-Adapted Physiology
biologicalTruth.desc: Your species evolved for the vacuum-adjacent environments of early spacefaring. When you Assess an environment, you may ask additional questions about heat differentials, atmospheric composition, pressure hazards, energy signatures, and structural integrity — even if you would not normally be entitled to those questions.

speciesTrait.name: Intuitive Navigation
speciesTrait.desc: You cannot get lost. Whether in hyperspace, on a planet's surface, or inside a labyrinthine station, you always maintain an innate sense of your position and heading. Additionally, when plotting a course through a hazardous route, you may ask the GM one free question about the dangers ahead before committing.

loreAnchors: Frontier Navigator — The Western Reaches are poorly mapped and border the Unknown Regions. Duros astrogation skills are highly prized.
Void-Adapted — Extra Assess questions on environments: heat, air, pressure, energy, structural integrity.
Intuitive Navigation — Cannot get lost; free question on hazardous routes before committing.
_aiMeta.directives: Focus on their connection to the stars and ships. Emphasize that they have memorized secret, highly dangerous hyperspace routes that the Imperial Navy is too afraid to chart. Their void-adapted senses make them invaluable scouts and surveyors.

name: Kel Dor
lore: Kel Dor hail from Dorin, a world of helium and exotic gases where the air itself would kill most beings. Outside Dorin, every Kel Dor wears a breath mask and protective goggles — a constant reminder that they are visitors everywhere they go. But the mask hides something else: a species-wide attunement to the Force that runs deeper than training. Even Kel Dor who have never held a lightsaber or studied under a master can feel the currents of the living Force — a quiet awareness that something is watching, guiding, warning. In the Western Reaches, they serve as bounty hunters with uncanny instincts, frontier mediators, and wanderers drawn to places where the Force feels disturbed.

arenaShift.name: Keen Awareness
arenaShift.desc: Wits starts at D8. All other Arenas at D6. No penalties.

favoredDiscipline.desc: Choose one favored discipline (exploding die + Edge reroll).

biologicalTruth.name: Antiox Dependence
biologicalTruth.desc: You cannot breathe standard nitrogen/oxygen atmospheres. Without your Antiox Breath Mask and protective goggles, you begin to suffocate and are effectively blind. You begin play with this equipment at no cost, but losing it in the field is a life-threatening emergency — not an inconvenience. Your mask's vocoder amplifies your voice, giving it a distinctive filtered resonance that is impossible to disguise.

speciesTrait.name: Force Touched
speciesTrait.desc: All Kel Dor possess an innate connection to the Force, regardless of training. You may always use the Sense discipline for an Assess maneuver, even if your Sense is at D4 and would normally be too low to attempt it. You can feel disturbances in the Force, search your feelings for guidance, and sense the presence of strong Force users — though without formal training, these impressions come as instinct, not knowledge.

loreAnchors: Antiox Dependence — Cannot breathe standard atmospheres; mask and goggles are life-or-death equipment, not cosmetic.
Force Touched — Innate Force connection; may always use Sense for Assess even at D4. Impressions come as instinct, not trained knowledge.
Filtered Voice — Vocoder-amplified speech is distinctive and impossible to disguise; everyone knows when a Kel Dor is speaking.
_aiMeta.directives: The character's backstory should treat the breath mask as both vulnerability and identity. Their Force sensitivity should manifest as instinct — gut feelings, uneasy premonitions, an uncanny read on strangers — not as Jedi training. Frame their presence in the Western Reaches as driven by something they can feel but not fully explain.

name: Rodian
lore: Rodians evolved on Rodia, a humid jungle world teeming with predators where every generation had to earn its survival through the hunt. This crucible forged a culture that glorifies the stalk, the kill, and the trophy — their art, their festivals, their social hierarchy all revolve around who can bring down the most dangerous prey. Since reaching the stars, Rodians have channeled this obsession into bounty hunting, mercenary work, and freelance tracking. In the Western Reaches, a Rodian with a contract is as reliable as gravity — they don't stop, they don't get bored, and they don't lose the trail. What they lack in social grace they make up for in single-minded tenacity. Most species make small talk; Rodians make kill marks.

arenaShift.name: Hunter's Reflexes
arenaShift.desc: Reflex starts at D8. Presence drops to D4. Lightning-quick and deadly accurate, but blunt, abrasive, and socially graceless.

favoredDiscipline.desc: Choose one favored discipline (exploding die + Edge reroll).

biologicalTruth.name: Infrared Vision
biologicalTruth.desc: Your multifaceted eyes see deep into the infrared spectrum, allowing you to operate in near-total darkness and detect heat signatures through thin cover. You ignore concealment from darkness (though not total darkness or solid barriers). At close range, you can distinguish warm-blooded beings from their surroundings even when they are partially hidden — a warm body behind a curtain, a figure crouched in shadow, breath fog in a cold corridor.

speciesTrait.name: The Hunt
speciesTrait.desc: When you declare a specific individual as your quarry (a named NPC, a specific bounty target, or a clearly identified enemy), all Survival checks to track that quarry and all Investigation checks to gather information about them are [Optimized] for the remainder of the session or until the quarry is captured, killed, or escapes off-world. You may only have one active quarry at a time. Declaring a new quarry ends the previous designation. This reflects the Rodian cultural obsession — once a hunt begins, it consumes your focus entirely.

loreAnchors: Infrared Vision — Multifaceted eyes see in darkness and detect heat signatures through thin cover.
The Hunt — Declaring a quarry grants [Optimized] on all tracking and investigation related to that target for the session.
Social Bluntness — Rodians are direct, abrasive, and prone to intimidation over diplomacy. Their Presence D4 reflects this.
_aiMeta.directives: The character's backstory should center around what they're hunting and why. A Rodian without a quarry is restless, agitated, purposeless — the hunt IS the identity. Frame their social difficulties not as stupidity but as predatory directness; they see social niceties as wasted motion. Their infrared vision should color how they describe the world — they notice heat, movement, and life signs before they notice faces or decorations.

name: Sullustan
lore: Sullustans evolved in the labyrinthine cave networks beneath the volcanic surface of Sullust, a world where getting lost meant death and seeing in the dark meant survival. These subterranean origins gave them enormous dark-adapted eyes, an unerring sense of direction, and an almost instinctive understanding of three-dimensional space. When they emerged into galactic civilization, they took to starships the way they once took to caves — as natural environments to navigate at speed. Sullustans are renowned pilots, engineers, and navigators, with the SoroSuub Corporation standing as one of the largest non-Human corporations in the galaxy. In the Western Reaches, Sullustans are the ones you want in the cockpit when the asteroid field closes in, and the ones you want at the cantina table when the mood needs lightening — they're gregarious, pragmatic, and famously fond of practical jokes.

arenaShift.name: Cave-Born Acuity
arenaShift.desc: Wits starts at D8. Physique drops to D4. Brilliant navigators with acute senses, but small-framed and physically fragile.

favoredDiscipline.desc: Choose one favored discipline (exploding die + Edge reroll).

biologicalTruth.name: Darkvision
biologicalTruth.desc: Your large, cave-adapted eyes can see in total darkness. You perceive shapes, depth, and movement with perfect clarity even in the complete absence of light — though you lose color perception in true darkness, seeing only in shades of grey. This is not low-light vision or thermal sensing; you see in the dark the way most beings see in daylight. Sudden bright light can be momentarily overwhelming.

speciesTrait.name: Spatial Memory
speciesTrait.desc: You possess a perfect biological sense of direction. Any path you have consciously traveled, you can retrace — even in total darkness, even years later. You always know which direction you are facing and how to get back to where you started, provided you were aware of the journey. If you were unconscious, drugged, or otherwise unaware of how you arrived somewhere, your spatial memory has nothing to work with — you are as lost as anyone else until you reach a location you recognize. Under normal circumstances, a Sullustan simply cannot get lost. This is not a skill; it is how your brain is wired.

loreAnchors: Darkvision — Full vision in total darkness; evolved underground. Color-blind only in absolute dark.
Spatial Memory — Perfect direction sense; can retrace any consciously traveled path. Cannot be made lost under normal circumstances.
Gregarious Engineer Culture — Pleasant, joke-loving, pragmatic; SoroSuub Corporation background; natural tinkerers.
_aiMeta.directives: The character's backstory should reflect their relationship with enclosed spaces, navigation, and machinery. A Sullustan is most comfortable in a cockpit, a maintenance shaft, or a cave — open skies and wide horizons can feel unsettling (though not debilitating). Their humor should come through as a coping mechanism and social tool. Frame their Western Reaches presence around a ship, a route, or a mechanical problem that needs solving.

name: Togruta
tagline: Pack Hunters. Sharp Senses. Never Alone.
lore: Togruta evolved as communal predators on Shili, a world of dense forests and meter-tall turu-grass where survival demanded teamwork, stealth, and spatial awareness. Their striped horns, montrals, and vivid skin patterns aren't decoration — they're camouflage, evolved to stalk prey through red-and-white scrubland. Togruta culture prizes the group over the individual, but their greatest leaders always emerge through fierce personal initiative. In the Western Reaches, Togruta who leave Shili call it "hunting among the stars" — and they bring the pack instinct with them. They gravitate toward crews, posses, and tight-knit teams, becoming the member who always knows where everyone is and who moves first when the ambush springs.

arenaShift.name: Predator's Agility
arenaShift.desc: Reflex starts at D8. Physique drops to D4. Quick and agile, but lean-framed for their size.

favoredDiscipline.desc: Choose one favored discipline (exploding die + Edge reroll).

biologicalTruth.name: Montral Echolocation
biologicalTruth.desc: Your hollow montrals function as passive echolocation organs, giving you spatial awareness of your surroundings independent of sight or sound. You can sense the position of nearby beings and objects even in total darkness, through smoke, or around corners — as long as they are within close range. This does not grant you detail (you can't read a datapad through a wall), but you always know if something is there and roughly where it is.

speciesTrait.name: Pack Hunter
speciesTrait.desc: After a successful Join Battle roll is resolved, you may swap your initiative position with one willing ally for the remainder of combat. You designate that ally as your hunting partner. For the duration, if you attack or take any action against the same target your hunting partner is engaged with, you are [Optimized] for that action. If your hunting partner is also a Togruta, both characters gain the [Optimized] benefit when acting against a shared target. This effect is broken if either character is rendered unconscious or otherwise cannot take an appropriate action (such as attacking). [Optimized] from Pack Hunter does not stack with other [Optimized] effects.

loreAnchors: Montral Echolocation — Passive spatial awareness at close range; can sense beings through darkness, smoke, and around corners.
Pack Hunter — Initiative swap with ally after Join Battle; [Optimized] when acting against shared target. Both get it if both are Togruta.
Communal Predator Culture — Togruta value the group over the individual; leaving Shili is called "hunting among the stars."
_aiMeta.directives: The character's backstory should reflect the tension between communal identity and individual ambition. Their montrals should be described as an ever-present background awareness — footsteps behind walls, heartbeats in the next room, the shape of a room felt rather than seen. Frame their presence in the Western Reaches as someone who seeks or has found a new pack — a crew, a cause, a community that needs a predator's instincts.

name: Twi'lek
lore: Twi'leks are among the galaxy's most exploited peoples — prized by slavers, objectified by the powerful, and scattered across every spaceport and cantina from the Core to the Rim. But beneath the galaxy's assumptions lies a culture of fierce resilience, silent communication, and razor-sharp social instincts honed by centuries of survival under the boots of others.

arenaShift.name: The Baseline
arenaShift.desc: All Arenas at D6. No biological peaks or penalties.

favoredDiscipline.desc: Choose one favored discipline (exploding die + Edge reroll).

biologicalTruth.name: Lekku
biologicalTruth.desc: Your head-tails are sensory organs attuned to emotional resonance. When you Assess a living target, you may ask additional questions about their emotional state, whether they are being truthful, and whether they are concealing intent — even if you would not normally be entitled to those questions.

speciesTrait.name: Pheromonal Influence
speciesTrait.desc: The first time per scene you attempt a Charm or Deception action against a given living target while face-to-face, you are treated as [Optimized] on that roll. This does not stack with other [Optimized] effects. Has no effect on droids or targets without biological scent receptors.

loreAnchors: Subject to Imperial Marginalization — Aliens are treated as second-class citizens or cheap labor under the New Order.
Lekku Empathy — Can read emotional states and detect lies through sensory lekku, making them devastating interrogators and negotiators.
Pheromonal Influence — First Charm/Deception per target per scene is [Optimized], face-to-face, living targets only.
_aiMeta.directives: Emphasize the character's reliance on lekku-based perception and pheromonal influence to survive in dangerous social environments. Frame their presence in the Western Reaches as either an escape from exploitation or a hustle to stay ahead of the syndicates.

name: Wookiee
lore: Kashyyyk is occupied. The Empire enslaves Wookiees by the thousands for brutal labor in spice mines, weapons factories, and frontier mining operations. A free Wookiee in the Western Reaches is either a fugitive, a survivor, or someone with very powerful friends. They are walking targets — but they are also walking siege engines, master mechanics, and holders of life-debts that transcend death itself.

arenaShift.name: Raw Mass
arenaShift.desc: Physique starts at D8. Reflex drops to D4. Massive and powerful, but lumbering.

favoredDiscipline.desc: Choose one favored discipline (exploding die + Edge reroll).

biologicalTruth.name: Shyriiwook
biologicalTruth.desc: You cannot speak Basic or any other language that requires human-range vocal anatomy. You understand Basic perfectly, but you can only speak Shyriiwook. Communicating with non-Wookiees requires a translator — organic, droid, or otherwise.

speciesTrait.name: Wookiee Rage
speciesTrait.desc: When a sacred bond is threatened — a life-debt, a loved one, a deeply held oath — you may enter Wookiee Rage. While raging: all Physique actions are treated as Unleash (maximum effort, no holding back), you immediately burn ALL accumulated Trauma, and you cannot voluntarily end the rage until the threat is resolved or you are incapacitated.

loreAnchors: Imperial Enslavement Target — Kashyyyk is occupied. Wookiees are actively hunted by the Empire for brutal labor.
Shyriiwook — Cannot speak Basic; requires a translator to communicate with most species.
Wookiee Rage — Sacred bond threatened triggers Unleash on all Physique, burns ALL Trauma, cannot voluntarily end.
_aiMeta.directives: The character's backstory must reflect the immediate, existential danger of being a Wookiee under the Empire. Do not treat them as a standard citizen; they are a walking target. The Wookiee Rage mechanic should feel sacred and terrifying, not casual.

name: Zabrak
lore: Zabrak culture venerates self-reliance, physical endurance, and refusal to submit. Their homeworld of Iridonia is a crucible of lethal environments that breeds toughness into every generation. In the Western Reaches, Zabrak are found as mercenaries, frontier settlers, and resistance fighters — anyone who needs to survive where others would break.

arenaShift.name: The Baseline
arenaShift.desc: All Arenas at D6. No biological peaks or penalties.

favoredDiscipline.desc: Choose one favored discipline (exploding die + Edge reroll).

biologicalTruth.name: Redundant Biology
biologicalTruth.desc: You possess a secondary heart and a nervous system with an extraordinarily high pain threshold. When you take Physique or Reflex Trauma, you soak 3 instead of the normal 2 — your body simply absorbs more punishment before degrading.

speciesTrait.name: Unyielding
speciesTrait.desc: Once per round, you may spend a Maneuver to throw off one ongoing mental condition — fear, confusion, despair, [Shaken], or any similar effect. The condition still lands and applies on the round it hits, but you recover faster than anyone expects. Your cultural conditioning and biological pain tolerance let you grit your teeth and push through what would paralyze others.

loreAnchors: Redundant Biology — Physique/Reflex Trauma soaks 3 instead of 2; built to absorb punishment.
Unyielding — Once per round, spend a Maneuver to throw off one ongoing mental condition (fear, [Shaken], confusion, despair, etc.).
Defiant Culture — Culturally resistant to authoritarian rule, natural enemies of Imperial governors.
_aiMeta.directives: Highlight their physical resilience and stubborn pride. Emphasize a background of enduring extreme physical hardship. The Unyielding trait costs a Maneuver — it is not free immunity, it is the Zabrak gritting their teeth and shaking it off through effort and will.

name: Dathomirian
lore: The Dathomirians are a hybrid species born from generations of interbreeding between human women and male Zabraks on the Dark Side-steeped world of Dathomir. The females — the Nightsisters — present as human but carry Zabrak resilience beneath the surface: hardened constitutions, heightened pain tolerance, and a biological attunement to the dark energies that saturate their homeworld. After General Grievous massacred the Nightsister clans during the Clone Wars, the survivors scattered across the galaxy — hiding what they are, carrying their traditions in whispered rituals and muscle memory.

arenaShift.name: The Baseline
arenaShift.desc: All Arenas at D6. No biological peaks or penalties.

favoredDiscipline.desc: Choose one favored discipline (exploding die + Edge reroll).

biologicalTruth.name: Hybrid Resilience
biologicalTruth.desc: Beneath a human exterior, your body carries the redundant nervous pathways and enhanced pain threshold of your Zabrak ancestry. When you take Physique Trauma, you soak 3 instead of the normal 2.

speciesTrait.name: Witch-Blood
speciesTrait.desc: Generations of brewing and ingesting Nightsister potions have chemically hardened your bloodline. You are naturally resistant to poisons, toxins, and chemical agents. When you would take damage or suffer a condition from a poison, toxin, or chemical source, the effect is [Weakened] — reduce damage by one step or downgrade the condition severity by one tier. This resistance is biological, not magical — it functions whether or not you practice Magick.

loreAnchors: Hybrid Resilience — Physique Trauma soaks 3 instead of 2; Zabrak toughness beneath human appearance.
Witch-Blood — Innate resistance to poisons, toxins, and chemical agents; effects are [Weakened].
Nightsister Survivor — One of the few remaining after General Grievous's massacre; hiding in plain sight across the galaxy.
Dark Side Attunement — Biological connection to Dathomir's dark energies; does not require Force training to manifest as instinct and resilience.
_aiMeta.directives: Emphasize the tension between human appearance and alien heritage. She looks human but she is not — her blood carries something older and darker. Nightsister survivors in 16 BBY are hunted relics of a dead culture. Lean into isolation, secrecy, and the weight of carrying traditions that have no home. The Witch-Blood trait is passive and biological — it is not Magick, it is what Magick did to her ancestors over centuries.
